title: Understanding Data Quality Dimensions
text: Data quality dimensions are criteria used to assess the quality of data. They include aspects like completeness, uniqueness, consistency, and validity. Each dimension addresses a different aspect of data quality.

title: Focus on the Date
text: Consider the birth date '4/3/2200'. Think about what makes this date problematic. Is it the format, the values, or something else?

title: What is Validity?
text: Validity refers to whether the data values fall within the acceptable range or domain. For example, a birth date should be within a reasonable range of years.

title: Identify the Problem
text: Given the birth date '4/3/2200', identify which data quality dimension is being violated.
